instruction.title: Preparation of Chicken:
steps: Wash and pat dry chicken pieces. Prick lightly with a fork for deeper marinade penetration.

instruction.title: Roasting the Gram Flour (Besan):
steps: Dry roast 2 tbsp besan on medium heat until golden & aromatic. Cool and set aside.

instruction.title: Marinade:
steps: In a bowl, combine yogurt, cream, ginger-garlic paste, chili paste, lemon juice, mustard oil, roasted besan, and all spices. Mix well.
Add chicken cubes, coat evenly, cover, and refrigerate minimum 4 hours, preferably overnight.

instruction.title: Cooking Options:
steps: Charcoal/BBQ (Authentic): Skewer marinated chicken and grill on medium heat, basting with butter/ghee until charred and cooked through (8–12 min).
Oven: Preheat to 220°C (425°F). Place skewered chicken on a rack over a tray. Bake 15–18 min, turning once. Broil (grill mode) for 2–3 min for charred edges.
Tandoor (Professional): Cook at 300°C until smoky, tender, and charred.

instruction.title: Finishing:
steps: Once cooked, brush with melted butter/ghee for shine & flavor.
Serve hot with onion rings, lemon wedges, and mint chutney.
Use chicken thighs – they stay moist, unlike breast which can dry out.
Double marination – first with lemon, chili, salt (20 min), then with yogurt-spice mix (overnight). This boosts depth of flavor.
Smoky finish – If not BBQing, use the dhungar method: place a hot charcoal piece in a small bowl inside the chicken tray, pour ghee over it, cover tightly for 5 min. This adds tandoori smokiness.
Serve immediately – Boti kababs dry out if left standing too long.
